[2018-03-14 08:24] p0lari (Polari): hello I would like to vote in favour of draconian moderation
[2018-03-14 08:24] p0lari (Polari): I've gone through this argument in a couple of other places already, but basically you can't foster non-awful discussion through objective rules and by enforcing superficial politeness
[2018-03-14 08:30] Deleted User (Deleted User): so what would the reasoning be for the banning/throttling if you do it to him say
[2018-03-14 08:32] p0lari (Polari): sorry got interrupted by something at work
[2018-03-14 08:32] p0lari (Polari): the reasoning would be that you accept that you can't cover all harmful behaviour under objective rules and allow mods to act more freely
[2018-03-14 08:33] p0lari (Polari): if someone's determined to be an annoying piece of shit and you commit to only enforcing objective rules, they will get around them
[2018-03-14 08:33] Deleted User (Deleted User): so basically the ban/throttle is "because we say so"?
[2018-03-14 08:33] p0lari (Polari): your content is garbage, stop that shit
[2018-03-14 08:35] mtanzer: I'm also in favor of 'the ends justify the means'
[2018-03-14 08:35] mqp: you don't ban him or throttle him, you tell him that he's kind of dominating the conversation in the channel and will he please try to let up a little bit in between successive units and revisions to give other people room to think
[2018-03-14 08:35] p0lari (Polari): I mean sure, that should be the first step
[2018-03-14 08:36] p0lari (Polari): then if he still keeps spamming garbage, then you take forceful action
[2018-03-14 08:36] mtanzer: Yeah the first thing to do if anything is done is to talk to him about why there's a problem and ask him to adjust
[2018-03-14 08:36] mqp: then he says either oh, ok, sorry about that or he sits there arguing with you that he is being right and just and refuses to change at all in which case you silence him for a while or ban him, i don't know what kind of tools you have
[2018-03-14 08:40] mqp: (punf is an example of someone who seems to immediately respond to feedback like this by claiming that he is being right and just and demanding that you point to the rule he broke and giving examples of how it's unfair, etc., i would be interested to hear from his perspective how he feels about it because he otherwise seems to me like a smart person and i am surprised by this trait)
[2018-03-14 08:43] Deleted User (Deleted User): because he's obsessed with rules
[2018-03-14 08:44] Deleted User (Deleted User): that's why he loves shit such as "always gambit" "shadowfang sucks" "lucina is overpowered" "first antima wins" etc
[2018-03-14 08:49] p0lari (Polari): right, if we do actually adopt mods having full freedom to act I don't expect everyone to like it
[2018-03-14 08:49] p0lari (Polari): but I've seen enough examples of the opposite not working
[2018-03-14 08:50] p0lari (Polari): you get some clueless, obstinate people who argue forever with knowledgeable people, knowledgeable people get sick of it and stop participating, clueless people remain
[2018-03-14 08:50] p0lari (Polari): and eventually every discussion being garbage just becomes something everyone knows and agrees, and maybe even gets to meme level
[2018-03-14 08:51] p0lari (Polari): at which point it's probably not even salvageable anymore
[2018-03-14 08:51] Deleted User (Deleted User): what's the examples in just wondering
[2018-03-14 08:52] p0lari (Polari): mtg circles were my main thing for a long while and mtg salvation is like the canonical example
[2018-03-14 08:53] p0lari (Polari): here's a really nice post about it http://blog.killgold.fish/2011/08/why-is-mtg-salvation-bad.html
why is mtg salvation bad?
Pt 1: Overview   Commonly-held opinions don’t always get written down. Not in any sort of persuasive form, at least. Usually just in passing...
[2018-03-14 08:53] p0lari (Polari): and we had some pretty big arguments about this at mtgsuomi ("mtgfinland"), which went downhill really bad over the years
[2018-03-14 08:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): what is mtg salvation? as in what is it in relation to mtg
[2018-03-14 08:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): I guess I could google it eh
[2018-03-14 08:54] p0lari (Polari): sirlin's older forums had a very laissez-faire moderation policy and it worked as long as the population was small and didn't have big offenders, but the few problem persons it did have were a fucking disaster
[2018-03-14 08:54] p0lari (Polari): mtg salvation is a forum
[2018-03-14 08:55] p0lari (Polari): looking at the site I guess not only a forum? but the forum is what I think of when I hear mtgs
[2018-03-14 08:57] p0lari (Polari): but outside larger examples of a whole forum driven to the ground, you just see it all the time on a smaller scale
[2018-03-14 08:57] Deleted User (Deleted User): >It’s no surprise that the only Magic “celebrity” that goes near MTGS is the one who most embodies its enthusiasm, comradery, and cluelessness, Evan Erwin.

idk who he is, but this sentence is lulz
[2018-03-14 08:57] p0lari (Polari): like on any card game forum, bads make threads about their horrible decks, good players tell them what they are doing wrong, and they refuse to listen and argue back about shit they are absolutely clueless about
[2018-03-14 08:58] p0lari (Polari): and they never learn, and it never changes
[2018-03-14 08:58] p0lari (Polari): and the thing is, if you only enforce the kind of superficial politeness that is easy to write objective rules for, they are doing nothing wrong, and if the good players get fed up with them and tell them to get stuffed, *that*'s what breaks the rules
[2018-03-14 08:59] p0lari (Polari): which is extremely frustrating
[2018-03-14 08:59] Deleted User (Deleted User): yeah I think you either need to have rules lax enough to allow negativity, or harsh enough to not allow stupidity
[2018-03-14 08:59] Deleted User (Deleted User): if i'm interpreting right?
[2018-03-14 09:00] p0lari (Polari): "allowing negativity" as you put it is imo better than not allowing it but allowing stupidity, but it's just going to end up in a shitstorm
[2018-03-14 09:00] Deleted User (Deleted User): what exactly is "mod text" do you know?
[2018-03-14 09:00] p0lari (Polari): which at least is fun for a little while, instead of the stupidity perpetuating and becoming the norm faster

[2018-03-14 09:01] Deleted User (Deleted User): I feel it's generally pretty low, though people like punf/me/yuj have it quite high

[2018-03-14 17:13] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): So it comes down to "what is the goal of this Discord: to encourage mature discussions between people already familiar with Prismata or to have a positive place for all levels of players to engage with the game/community"

Maybe you can have both, but need to gradually increase the quality of posts expected from people as they become more experienced. And that is where SSP falls short.

[2018-03-17 06:00] http404error: Am I still doing okay? I feel bad about arguing about SDR so much today... it's just a complex and highly nuanced topic (not to mention one of widely overestimated value). Very hard to explain.
[2018-03-17 06:01] http404error: I definitely err on the side of "ensuring correct information is dispersed" over "preserving cool tempers in others"
[2018-03-17 06:02] Deleted User (Deleted User): I think kids semi-cba'd a decent #(arguably, more than they should)
[2018-03-17 06:06] http404error: Yeah, I think the root of the issue is the theme choice, that's easy to see.
[2018-03-17 06:07] http404error: I don't really want to make people care about SDR if they don't want to!
[2018-03-17 06:08] http404error: And on one hand, I can understand that many users don't have a reason to trust my assertions, because I'm not a super child prodigy MIT dropout.
[2018-03-17 06:09] http404error: But yeah, I'm surprised how vehemently some users care about their incomplete understanding of something that doesn't affect their life!
[2018-03-17 06:12] Deleted User (Deleted User): nerdy mental masturbation tbh
[2018-03-17 06:15] liadahlia (Lia): people hate being wrong
[2018-03-17 06:17] http404error: True. Also true. I guess I underestimate pride.
[2018-03-17 06:21] http404error: I just don't yet know if I could have or should have approached it differently
[2018-03-17 06:23] liadahlia (Lia): try to respect their opinion even if it's wrong or otherwise
[2018-03-17 06:23] wilson0862 (Wilson): So personally, I do think it's important to care about people's feelings and stuff but I think at some point, that matters less than... not being correct per se, but expressing true things.
[2018-03-17 06:24] Deleted User (Deleted User): i don't understand what's this abt now
[2018-03-17 06:24] wilson0862 (Wilson): Me neither. I should say upfront that I don't have any context at all.
[2018-03-17 06:24] http404error: You wouldn't understand, don't concern yourself with it 😛
[2018-03-17 06:24] http404error: I'm just joking. But seriously, it's not worth reading the logs
[2018-03-17 06:24] wilson0862 (Wilson): But as a theoretical conversation, that's my general stance.
[2018-03-17 06:25] wilson0862 (Wilson): Like I personally don't subscribe to the notion that everybody's opinion is valid quite frankly. Again, please don't view my assertion in the context of whatever the actual tangible situation you are talking about, lmao
[2018-03-17 06:26] wilson0862 (Wilson): Like there are some opinions which people take the time and effort to develop and there are opinions which people... don't.
[2018-03-17 06:26] http404error: I mean, you're right that someone can just be flat wrong, obviously.
[2018-03-17 06:26] http404error: The question is... sometimes correcting someone isn't worth it.
[2018-03-17 06:26] liadahlia (Lia): just keep in mind that *you* can be wrong
[2018-03-17 06:26] wilson0862 (Wilson): Always. I'm always happy to be shown to be wrong.
[2018-03-17 06:26] liadahlia (Lia): you might think someone's wrong but the other person also thinks you're wrong
[2018-03-17 06:27] http404error: Well naturally that's how the situation arose at all. Otherwise there's no argument.
[2018-03-17 06:27] wilson0862 (Wilson): Absolutely and like I said, I respect the thought and investment in reaching an opinion.
[2018-03-17 06:27] wilson0862 (Wilson): Not every opinion intrinsically.
[2018-03-17 06:27] wilson0862 (Wilson): There is a difference between showing respect and withholding your thoughts though imo.
[2018-03-17 06:28] wilson0862 (Wilson): Of course if you don't think it's productive to even go into the conversation, then use your judgment.
[2018-03-17 06:28] http404error: I think to some extent conflict was unavoidable because I kind of have to make a ruling on what SDR is to make it the contest theme!
[2018-03-17 06:30] liadahlia (Lia): like, i don't necessarily agree with your definition of SDR but that's fine really
[2018-03-17 06:30] http404error: I think it's really interesting to nose down into the nuances of the differences in our interpretations!
[2018-03-17 06:30] http404error: Other users kind of got really heated about it though.
[2018-03-17 06:30] http404error: It's mostly just very hard to explain the deep stuff.
[2018-03-17 06:31] http404error: I keep thinking "if only I could have explained it better"
[2018-03-17 06:31] http404error: And I'm questioning if that's just a mental trap
[2018-03-17 06:31] http404error: I definitely used to have the exact position that every single user who has disagreed with me has had, at one point in the past.
[2018-03-17 06:32] http404error: I've been thinking about Shalev for literally years, even between the long spans I wasn't playing Prismata.
[2018-03-17 06:33] http404error: So it's not like I don't understand! And even I have had my eyes opened to some weird-ass new things today just talking and thinking about it more.
[2018-03-17 06:36] wilson0862 (Wilson): I dunno, maybe unit creation is just a more personal thing than I would've originally thought, haha
[2018-03-17 06:37] http404error: Oh I expected *that* part of it.
[2018-03-17 06:37] http404error: I'm expecting people to learn over time that they should be less attached to their designs so that they can improve better.
[2018-03-17 06:37] http404error: Or to... not learn that, and just never win the contests.
[2018-03-17 06:38] http404error: Hey, even bad designers can hit a lucky streak every now and then, so it's not all hopeless.

[2018-03-19 17:51] willmasaur (Will Ma): I don't know if this has been discussed before, but one barrier for new players I feel is that the jump from Campaign to "1v1  Play" (vs. either Bot or Human) is too large
[2018-03-19 17:52] willmasaur (Will Ma): I think this can be solved by having a "restricted 1v1 mode" where the base set is DEBSWAT (no Rhino, or Green units)
[2018-03-19 17:53] willmasaur (Will Ma): The random set units can be Gilded Drone, Drake, Odin, Omega, Shadowfang, Advocate, etc.. and maybe like 3 are chosen each game or something
[2018-03-19 17:54] willmasaur (Will Ma): And players can match both vs. other players and vs. bots
[2018-03-19 17:55] willmasaur (Will Ma): This allows players to get a taste of real Prismata before even finishing Episode 1 + learning how Green works, which a lot of players aren't patient enough to do
[2018-03-19 18:00] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): which is the best, sorry? The prismata party, or one without Arkanishu
